strengths: Clearly identifies three specific, personal real-world objects and situates each in its everyday context.
Each object is linked to the IA prompt with a coherent explanation of how it functions as a material tool to produce or acquire knowledge.
Commentaries offer sustained justification for the inclusion of each object, repeatedly referring to key TOK concepts (production, acquisition of knowledge).
Original photographs and personal anecdotes enhance authenticity and anchor the exhibition in lived experience.
Acknowledgement of counterclaims (e.g., subjectivity of interpretation, potential misuse of tools) demonstrates emerging critical awareness.
weaknesses: Explanations lack precision and depth; many claims are asserted rather than critically examined.
Analysis remains largely descriptive, with significant repetition across objects and few distinct, well-developed arguments.
Supporting evidence is mostly anecdotal; sources are listed but not integrated through in-text citations.
Insufficient use of explicit TOK terminology (Ways of Knowing, Areas of Knowledge) to frame the discussion.
Structural issues hinder clarity (overlong paragraphs, abrupt transitions, incomplete sentences).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The studentâs exhibition meets the descriptors for the top of the 5â6 band: three specific objects are clearly identified, each linked to the IA prompt, and justified with some supporting evidence. Strengths include authentic personal context, coherent links to the prompt, and acknowledgement of counterclaims. However, imprecision in analysis, descriptive rather than analytical treatment, uneven integration of evidence, and limited use of TOK terminology prevent the work from reaching the 7â8 band. This balance of solid achievement with significant areas for improvement warrants a final score of 6.

annotations.comment: Including an original photograph of the microscope enhances authenticity and strengthens the link between object and personal experience.
The assertion that the microscope enabled the âfirst observation of cell structureâ overgeneralizes historical development; focus instead on how it extends your own perceptual capabilities.
Your commentary remains largely descriptive and could be deepened by introducing explicit TOK terminology such as âperceptionâ or âways of knowingâ.
This section conflates reliability and certainty. In TOK, reliability of tools and epistemic certainty are distinctâclarify how they differ.
You note calculator misuse but lack real-world examples. Incorporating a case study (e.g., data entry errors) would ground this counterclaim.
Object 3âs introduction signals a clear shift to a different AOK (history), demonstrating awareness of how material tools function across disciplines.
The personal photo memoir places the object within a familial and historical context, enhancing the exhibitionâs emotional resonance.
The IA prompt is clearly stated and immediately anchors the exhibition in a focused TOK question, demonstrating strong awareness of the task requirements.
Object 1 is a specific, personal item (âMy Schoolâs Microscopeâ), which solidly grounds your discussion in a real-world context as required for a high-level exhibition.
This paragraph is overly long and unfocused. Break it into clear claims and counterclaims to improve argument structure and readability.
The claim that cell theory became fact âbecause of the microscopeâ is simplistic. Consider discussing theory-ladenness and how interpretation influences acceptance of data.
There is a lack of integration of your listed sources. Cite specific references when discussing concepts like objectivity to strengthen evidential support.
Key ideas are repeated (e.g., tools provide evidence); consolidating these points will make room for deeper analysis and reduce redundancy.
You acknowledge individual interpretation shaped by prior knowledge, which introduces a valuable counterclaim about subjectivity in knowledge acquisition.
Commentary 2 is clearly signposted, maintaining a consistent structure that aids reader orientation throughout the exhibition.
The personal photograph of the graphing calculator effectively situates the tool in your mathematics classroom, reinforcing its real-world context.
Describing the calculator as guaranteeing âcertaintyâ oversimplifies mathematical proof and disregards potential input errors; examine the nature of certainty in TOK more critically.
Integrate TOK vocabularyâsuch as âWays of Knowing: reason and intuitionââto show deeper engagement with the framework rather than solely descriptive claims.
Several sentences are cut off, suggesting incomplete thoughts. Ensure each idea is fully articulated for clarity and coherence.
There is a break between the end of the calculator discussion and the start of the photo memoir text. Ensure smooth transitions to maintain coherence.
You link the photo memoir to the AOK of history and articulate how material tools serve as sources of evidenceâthis aligns well with the IA prompt.
Discussing the photo memoir as historiography would deepen your justification. Introduce concepts from theory of history to support your analysis.
The exploration of emotional engagement demonstrates insight into emotion as a Way of Knowing, enriching your understanding of knowledge motivation.
Explicitly reference TOK frameworks (Ways of Knowing, Areas of Knowledge) to tie your emotional analysis more directly to the course concepts.
The counterclaim about negative emotions hindering knowledge is promising; illustrate with a concrete example to make the point more compelling.
Including a bibliography shows good research practice and indicates engagement with secondary sources to inform your commentary.
Sources are listed but never cited in the body of your commentary. In-text citations (e.g., (Reed, 2008)) are needed to connect evidence to your arguments.
Citation formats vary across entries. Adopt a consistent style (MLA, APA, etc.) to enhance professionalism and clarity.
When discussing philosophical concepts like âcertainty,â integrate direct footnotes or in-text references to your bibliography to strengthen your analysis.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content: The exhibition does not reach the standard described by the other levels or does not use one of the IA prompts provided.

The exhibition presents
three objects, but the
real-world contexts of
these objects are not
stated, or the images
presented may be
highly generic images of
types of object rather
than of specific
real-world objects. Links
between the objects
and the selected IA
prompt are made, but
these are minimal,
tenuous, or it is not
clear what the student is
trying to convey.
There is very little
justification offered for
the inclusion of each
object in the exhibition.
The commentary on the
objects is highly
descriptive or consists
only of unsupported
assertions.

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of the objects may be implied rather than explicitly stated. Basic links between the objects and the selected IA prompt are made, but the explanation of these links is unconvincing and/or unfocused. There is a superficial justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Reasons for the inclusion of the objects are offered, but these are not supported by appropriate evidence and/or lack relevance to the selected IA prompt. There may be significant repetition across the justifications of the different objects

The exhibition identifies three objects, although the real-world contexts of these objects may be vaguely or imprecisely stated. There is some explanation of the links between the three objects and the selected IA prompt. There is some justification for the inclusion of each object in the exhibition. Some of the points are supported by evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition identifies three objects and their real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are explained, although this explanation may lack precision and clarity in parts. There is a justification of the contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. Many of the points are supported by appropriate evidence and references to the selected IA prompt.

The exhibition clearly identifies three objects and their specific real-world contexts. Links between each of the three objects and the selected IA prompt are clearly made and well-explained. There is a strong justification of the particular contribution that each individual object makes to the exhibition. All, or nearly all, of the points are well-supported by appropriate evidence and explicit references to the selected IA prompt.

rubricCriterionStrands.name: Does the exhibition successfully show how TOK manifests in the world around us?
